title: grumilea sylvatica
intro: Grumilea sylvatica, also known as the forest grumilea, is a flowering plant species in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to Australia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, including bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Grumilea sylvatica include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to create remedies for various health conditions.
